name: Moroccan Arabic
description: Darija is the Arabic spoken every day across Morocco — distinct enough from Modern Standard Arabic that MSA speakers often can't follow it unaided. This course teaches it the way it's actually written day to day: in Arabizi, the Latin-plus-numerals chat alphabet Moroccans use to text, post, and message.

script.name: Arabizi (Latin chat alphabet)
script.note: Darija is normally written in Arabic script, but everyday texting in Morocco overwhelmingly uses Arabizi: Latin letters plus a handful of digits standing in for sounds Latin script doesn't have — 3 for ع, 7 for ح, 9 for ق, and so on. This course teaches that form, since it's what you'll actually read and type.

tts.note: No eSpeak NG phonemizer reads Arabizi correctly, so live in-browser synthesis isn't accurate for this course — audio is meant to be pre-rendered only (Piper's Arabic voice, reading the real Arabic-script spelling behind each word, auto-diacritized by Piper's built-in tashkeel model — see each item's authoring-only "arabic" field and scripts/gen-audio.py). Until that generation step is run, Listen falls back to the browser's own system voice, which will likely mispronounce it.

sections.title: Beginner
sections.description: Greetings, numbers, questions, and first everyday vocabulary
